hyperedges.label: Nine-Agent Sequential Workflow Pipeline with WORKFLOW_STATE.md Handoff
Five Critical Plugin System Design Decisions Documented in WORKFLOW_STATE.md
SDD Implementation Phases A through J Tracked in PROGRESS.md
Multi-Agent Translation Pipeline Pattern
SDD Audit Documentation Ecosystem
Electron Security Baseline Triangle (sandbox + contextIsolation + nodeIntegration)
10-Agent Sequential Translation Pipeline
WorkflowEngine Dependency Injection Container
Quality Scoring → Calibration → QA Feedback Loop
Export Pipeline: Assembly → Conversion → Validation → Storage
Plugin Security: Manifest → Permissions → User Confirmation → API Gating
CI/CD Release: Build → Package → Sign → Upload Artifacts → Create Release

nodes.label: WORKFLOW_STATE.md as Shared Handoff
nodes.rationale: All agents share a single handoff file to preserve decisions, assumptions, blockers, and next steps across the sequential workflow. This avoids reliance on chat history as the only source of truth.

nodes.label: Context7 Library Verification
nodes.rationale: Agents must use context7 to verify library/framework/API behavior before proposing plans, during implementation, and during review. This prevents guessing library behavior from memory.

nodes.label: Nine-Agent Sequential Workflow
nodes.rationale: The team workflow follows a strict order: Planner → Debater → Implementor → Reviewer → Tester → Security-Reviewer → Linter → Commit-Message. This sequential gating ensures every change passes through clarification, critique, implementation, review, testing, security audit, linting, and commit message generation.

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Translation Pipeline
nodes.rationale: The translation pipeline uses 10 specialized AI agents (Split, Pré-trad, Traduire, Cohérence, Lexique, Grammaire, Style, Polish, QA, Export) to maintain consistency across 500+ chapters, powered by Translation Memory and a locked Lexicon. Each step is persisted in SQLite and individually retryable.

nodes.label: Conventional Commit Format
nodes.rationale: All commits use the conventional commit format (feat, fix, test, etc.) with optional body bullets. Commits are atomic (one task = one commit).

nodes.label: 26 SDD Documentation Volumes
nodes.rationale: The SDD (Software Design Document) contains 26 volumes covering Vision, Architecture, Testing, Security, CI/CD, and every subsystem. They serve as the canonical spec for all implementation.

nodes.label: Atomic Commits Pattern
nodes.rationale: Each implementation task produces exactly one atomic commit. This ensures git history is clean, revertable, and maps directly to individual SDD tasks.

nodes.label: Auto-Update Channels
nodes.rationale: Three update channels (latest, beta, alpha) determined by tag pattern: v2.0.2 → stable latest, v2.1.0-beta → beta pre-release, v2.1.0-alpha → alpha dev. The channel is embedded in latest.json for electron-updater.

nodes.label: Structured Logging (NDJSON)
nodes.rationale: The StructuredLogger class produces NDJSON output with redaction of sensitive keys, correlation IDs, and 1000-char message truncation. It implements child() and withCorrelationId() for composable logging.

nodes.label: Zod IPC Validation
nodes.rationale: All IPC handlers are validated with Zod schemas. This provides runtime type safety across the main-renderer boundary, replacing manual validation.

nodes.label: ESM Cache Busting for Plugin Hot-Reload
nodes.rationale: ESM modules are permanently cached by the module loader with no equivalent to delete require.cache. Solution: query-string cache busting in dev mode (import(`./plugin/index.mjs?t=${Date.now()}`)). In production, reload requires app restart.
nodes.norm_label: esm cache busting for plugin hot-reload

nodes.label: Electron Sandbox Security
nodes.rationale: SDD Volume 21 requires sandbox: true in webPreferences. The sandbox was changed from false to true as part of security hardening, complying with Electron's security best practices for renderer isolation.

nodes.label: Deferred Plugin Permissions Confirmation
nodes.rationale: PluginHost discovers plugins at startup, identifies those with sensitive permissions (project-write, fs-write, network), and defers activation until after createWindow() when the renderer can display a confirmation modal to the user. This solves the chicken-and-egg problem of needing UI before plugins are loaded.

nodes.label: ExportEngine Extensibility via customRenderers
nodes.rationale: ExportEngine gains a Map<ExportFormat, CustomRenderer> and public registerRenderer/unregisterRenderer methods. In render(), custom renderers are checked before the built-in switch, allowing plugins to add or override export formats without modifying ExportEngine source.

nodes.label: Electron net.fetch() for HTTP
nodes.rationale: All main-process HTTP calls use Electron's net.fetch() (Chrome network stack) instead of globalThis.fetch(). This resolves silent connection failures in desktop builds and is validated across OllamaManager, OllamaProvider, and RagEngine.

nodes.label: GitHub Releases Publishing
nodes.rationale: electron-builder publishes directly to GitHub Releases on tag push. The release workflow validates tag-vs-version consistency before building and publishing.

nodes.label: SDD Audit Priority Scale (🔴🟠🟡🔵🟢)
nodes.rationale: Five-tier priority system for SDD audit: critique/blocker, important, cosmetic, to-verify, good.

nodes.label: Pre-Implementation Claim Verification Principle
nodes.rationale: Claims marked 🔵 are non-blocking for design docs but must be validated before writing corresponding code.

nodes.label: Electron Security Sandbox Baseline
nodes.rationale: Modern Electron security: sandbox:true, contextIsolation:true, nodeIntegration:false via webPreferences. Replaces deprecated app.enableSandbox().

nodes.label: Phase 0 Validation Report — Ollama net.fetch() Migration
nodes.norm_label: phase 0 validation report — ollama net.fetch() migration

nodes.label: net.fetch() Migration from Global Fetch
nodes.rationale: Ollama HTTP layer migrated from globalThis.fetch() to Electron's net.fetch() with AbortSignal.timeout() and ReadableStream for NDJSON. Zero bare fetch() calls remain in src/main/.

nodes.label: Reuse-First Design Principle
nodes.rationale: For each core NovelTrad feature, identify an existing open source project from which to draw patterns or reuse, minimizing custom code.

nodes.label: debugLog() Function Duplication Issue (I1)
nodes.rationale: Identical debugLog() copy-pasted in 3 files (OllamaManager, router, handlers/ollama) writes to debug.log via fs.appendFileSync. Risk: interleaving on concurrent writes.

nodes.label: project:open Missing Zod Validation (I3)
nodes.rationale: project:open handler lacks Zod validation and path traversal check (assertWithinProject). Other handlers use assertWithinProject consistently.

nodes.label: Translation Memory vs Lexicon vs Cache Distinction
nodes.rationale: NovelTrad never uses 'cache'. Translation Memory (sentences) and Lexicon (terms) are persistent SQLite databases, not volatile caches. Three context levels: TM, Lexicon, RAG embeddings.
nodes.norm_label: translation memory vs lexicon vs cache distinction

nodes.label: EPUB Validation Pipeline (ZIP, OPF, epubcheck)
nodes.rationale: EPUB validation uses three checks: ZIP integrity, OPF metadata compliance, and epubcheck Java tool as subprocess.

nodes.label: Context Window 80% Safety Margin
nodes.rationale: 128K context window advertised for qwen3.5 with 80% safety margin and automatic chunking. Exact context size to be confirmed at runtime.

nodes.label: Workflow Retry and Fallback Pattern
nodes.rationale: 3 retries per step with exponential backoff, paused state for user correction, step-level and stage-level retry, fallback provider, circuit breaker after 3 failures.

nodes.label: Plugins v1.0 No-Sandbox Model
nodes.rationale: Plugins in v1.0 have no sandboxing. This is intentional — sandboxing deferred to future versions.

nodes.label: Convert Remaining [ ] Criteria to Automated Tests
nodes.rationale: Some volumes still have generic [ ] checklist criteria that must be converted to automated tests in CI during development.
nodes.norm_label: convert remaining [ ] criteria to automated tests

nodes.label: Volume 02 — Installation and First Launch Wizard
nodes.norm_label: volume 02 — installation and first launch wizard

nodes.label: Node.js-only Runtime (no Python)
nodes.rationale: Single runtime removes Python dependency, simplifies installation and packaging. Eliminates the PyQt6+FastAPI monolith of v4.

nodes.label: Reproducible Declarative Workflow
nodes.rationale: Every project is a self-contained folder (sources, lexicon, translations, cache, logs, SQLite). Workflow is observable, testable, with snapshots per step enabling retry/rollback.

nodes.label: contextBridge / Preload Script Security Pattern
nodes.rationale: contextIsolation=true, nodeIntegration=false, sandbox enabled, CSP strict. Only whitelisted APIs exposed via contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld().

nodes.label: Worker Threads for Agent Execution
nodes.rationale: Main process never does LLM work directly. Agents run in worker_threads to keep UI responsive. Configurable maxConcurrentJobs (default 1 for local Ollama).

nodes.label: Context Window Management Strategy
nodes.rationale: Tokens estimated with tiktoken. If prompt exceeds 50% of context window, auto-chunk chapter into coherent segments. 80% safety margin applied (128K window → ~100K usable).

nodes.label: Provider Fallback with Circuit Breaker
nodes.rationale: Principal → fallback on unavailability. 3 consecutive failures mark provider unavailable. Workflow pauses + user notification if both fail.

nodes.label: Repository Pattern (SQL Isolation)
nodes.rationale: SQL queries isolated in repositories/*.ts (Projects, Chapters, Lexicon, etc.) to separate persistence concerns from business logic.

nodes.label: Command Pattern for Workflow Steps
nodes.rationale: Each workflow step is a command with execute(), undo(), retry(). Enables retryStep and retryFrom without restarting the whole pipeline.

nodes.label: Sentence-Level TM Segmentation
nodes.rationale: TM works at sentence level (not paragraph) to maximize reuse. Paragraphs segmented by language-specific rules before storage (Chinese: 。！？；, Latin: .!?;).

nodes.label: Locked Lexicon Terms (Forced Translation)
nodes.rationale: locked=true terms cannot be translated differently. LexiconAgent enforces them imperatively. Aliases resolve to canonical entry. Priority determines application order for overlapping terms.

nodes.label: Forbidden Translation Terms
nodes.rationale: Inspired by NovelTrans and Glossarion. Optional forbidden[] array on lexicon entries lists translations that must never appear. LexiconAgent flags high-severity warnings if forbidden forms detected.

nodes.label: 7 Consistency Metrics (paragraphs, sentences, dialogues, length, named_entities, numbers, markup)
nodes.norm_label: 7 consistency metrics (paragraphs, sentences, dialogues, length, named_entities, numbers, markup)

nodes.label: Calibration Tolerances per Language Pair
nodes.rationale: Different language pairs have different information density (zh→fr expands, en→fr stays close). Tolerances calibrated: zh-fr length ratio 0.5-1.5, en-fr 0.8-1.2. Ratios account for CJK compression vs Latin expansion.

nodes.label: Quality Model Calibration (Linear Regression)
nodes.rationale: LLMs have scoring biases (some score 90+ systematically). Calibration via linear regression against 20-chapter human-annotated reference corpus. Coefficients stored per (model, dimension) in model_calibrations table. Recalibrate on model change or every 100 chapters.

nodes.label: 100% Local Mode (Full Offline Translation)
nodes.rationale: No external server required. Everything runs locally with Ollama. No source or translated data leaves the machine. SQLite + project files remain on local disk.

nodes.label: Agent Interface Contract (id, name, stage, execute)
nodes.norm_label: agent interface contract (id, name, stage, execute)

nodes.label: Job Step Snapshot Immutability Pattern
nodes.rationale: Each step produces an outputSnapshot that becomes the inputSnapshot of the next step. Immutability enables retry, rollback, and audit. retryStep uses the saved inputSnapshot to restart only that step.

nodes.label: retryStep / retryFrom Workflow Recovery
nodes.rationale: retryStep reruns a single step from its saved inputSnapshot. retryFrom restarts from a given stage. Configurable maxRetries per step (default 2). User can modify lexicon, prompt, model, or input text before retry.

nodes.label: Exponential Backoff + Circuit Breaker for Network
nodes.rationale: Network errors trigger exponential backoff. Ollama: 3 consecutive failures → mark provider unavailable + fallback. All errors inherit from NovelTradError, propagated to renderer as { type, code, message, details }.
nodes.norm_label: exponential backoff + circuit breaker for network

nodes.label: Import Encoding Detection Pipeline (UTF-8 → chardet → Latin-1 → user)
nodes.rationale: UTF-8 tested first via BOM. chardet as statistical fallback. Latin-1/Windows-1252 as final fallback. User confirmation if confidence < 0.7. All source/ files normalized to UTF-8.
nodes.norm_label: import encoding detection pipeline (utf-8 → chardet → latin-1 → user)

nodes.label: chapitres/ Archive vs source/ Working Copy Separation
nodes.rationale: chapitres/ holds immutable user-imported originals as reference archive. source/ holds normalized UTF-8 Markdown working copy. Split agent reads only source/, never chapitres/. This enables re-import if source/ gets corrupted.
nodes.norm_label: chapitres/ archive vs source/ working copy separation

nodes.label: Database Schema Migration Versioning
nodes.rationale: Migrations stored in src/main/db/migrations/. Each migration has version, name, up, down. MigrationRunner applies/reverts. Schema auto-migrates on project open if version mismatch.

nodes.label: v4 (PyQt6+FastAPI) to v2.0 (Electron+Vue3) Migration
nodes.rationale: v4 monolith (PyQt6+FastAPI Python) mixed UI, backend, agents, pipeline in single runtime. v2.0 separates concerns with Vue3 renderer, Node.js main process, worker threads, Ollama as sole local AI dependency.

nodes.label: ExportEngine
nodes.rationale: Central class orchestrating export: generate content per format, write to file, validate. Uses switch on ExportFormat enum. Default output path joins projectPath/exports/sanitizedTitle.ext.

nodes.label: Export Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Sequential flow: translated paragraphs + metadata → assembly → format conversion → write to exports/ → validation (size, epubcheck for EPUB) → insert into exports table → UI notification.

nodes.label: Bilingual Export Mode
nodes.rationale: Export each source paragraph followed by its translation. Inspired by epub-translator, bbook-maker, PolyglotShelf. Supported in EPUB (alternating paragraphs), HTML (semantic lang blocks), Markdown/TXT (>prefix). UI option with side-by-side preview.

nodes.label: EPUB Validation Chain
nodes.rationale: Three-tier validation: ZIP structural check (mimetype file first, META-INF/container.xml exists, no corruption), OPF metadata check (dc:title, dc:language, manifest, spine), optional epubcheck Java tool. ZIP+OPF mandatory; epubcheck blocking if installed, warning if absent.

nodes.label: Batch Export
nodes.rationale: Multi-chapter selection in UI; one file per chapter or single aggregated file for EPUB. Queue with progress indicator. EPUB multi-chapter maps selected chapters to epub-gen-memory chapters array.

nodes.label: Hybrid Snapshot Storage
nodes.rationale: Full snapshots at versions 1, 5, 10 and every 10 thereafter; incremental diffs for intermediate versions. Compress JSON with zlib if >10KB. Balances storage size against restore speed.

nodes.label: Diff Algorithm (Paragraph-level)
nodes.rationale: Paragraph-level diff comparing old vs new paragraph arrays; detects added, removed, and modified paragraphs. Line-level diff uses diff-match-patch for fine-grained text comparison.

nodes.label: Rollback Mechanism
nodes.rationale: User selects a version → diff preview → confirmation → replace current paragraphs → create new version vN+1 with triggeredBy:'rollback'. Partial rollback supported: select specific changed paragraphs from diff.

nodes.label: Audit Journal
nodes.rationale: Traces every action: project created/opened/deleted, chapter imported/translated/exported/rolled_back, workflow started/step_completed/step_failed, lexicon entry_added/entry_modified. Tracks userId, agentId, modelId, durationMs, tokens.

nodes.label: PluginHost
nodes.rationale: Loads plugins from directory: reads manifest.json, validates schema, checks permissions, loads module, calls activate(), registers contributions. Supports hot-reload in dev mode by watching plugins/ folder for changes.

nodes.label: Plugin Permissions Model
nodes.rationale: Explicit permissions (ai, lexicon, project-read, project-write, fs-read, fs-write, network, ui). Sensitive permissions require user confirmation. v1.0: plugins run in main process with same privileges; no V8 sandbox. v2.0: signed plugins, marketplace.

nodes.label: Plugin Hot-Reload (Dev Mode)
nodes.rationale: Watches plugins/ directory; on file change triggers unload + reload automatically. Must be disabled in production.

nodes.label: Plugin Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Discovery → manifest read → validation → user confirmation (if dangerous) → module load → activate() → contributions registered → usage → deactivate() → unload.

nodes.label: IPC Router
nodes.rationale: Central IPC registration in src/main/ipc/router.ts. Maps handlers to channels: project, workflow, lexicon, model, export, settings. Rejects unknown channels.

nodes.label: IPC Zod Payload Validation
nodes.rationale: Every IPC payload validated with Zod schemas before processing. Schemas in src/main/ipc/schemas/. Validation failure returns standard {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR', message, details}.

nodes.label: Preload Script (contextBridge)
nodes.rationale: Exposes novelTradAPI in renderer via contextBridge. Only declares whitelisted channels: createProject, openProject, listChapters, startWorkflow, pauseWorkflow, listLexicon, etc. Never exposes raw ipcRenderer.

nodes.label: IPC Error Handling Standard
nodes.rationale: Standard IpcError format: {code, message, details}. Zod errors return VALIDATION_ERROR. Business errors return BUSINESS_ERROR. Unexpected errors log stack trace on main, return generic message to renderer.

nodes.label: Shared TypeScript Interfaces
nodes.rationale: All business interfaces in packages/shared/src/types/ (Project, Chapter, Paragraph, etc.). Used by both main and renderer processes for type consistency.

nodes.label: UpdateManager
nodes.rationale: EventEmitter-based manager wrapping electron-updater. autoDownload=false, autoInstallOnAppQuit=false. Prompts user before download and install. Events: checking, available, not-available, progress, downloaded, error.

nodes.label: latest.json Manifest
nodes.rationale: Enriched update manifest with version, channel, release_date, download_url, SHA256, release_notes_url, mandatory flag, min_app_version. Generated by CI script, uploaded as release asset alongside electron-builder latest*.yml files.

nodes.label: Update Channels (stable/beta/alpha)
nodes.rationale: Three channels: stable (default all users), beta (voluntary testers), alpha (internal dev). electron-updater filters by semver pre-release tag. generateUpdatesFilesForAllChannels:true in electron-builder.yml.

nodes.label: Structured JSON Logging
nodes.rationale: JSON-structured file logs with fields: timestamp (ISO 8601), level, component, message, correlationId. Optional: durationMs, tokensIn/Out, error, projectId, chapterId. Files structured, Console UI shows human-readable format.

nodes.label: Crash Reports
nodes.rationale: Caught via process.on('uncaughtException'). Stored in crash-reports/ with stack trace and version. Opt-in auto-send.

nodes.label: Log Rotation Policy
nodes.rationale: Daily rotation. Retention: 7 days global logs, 30 days project logs. Four destinations: global logs/app.log, per-project logs/, dev console, Console UI.

nodes.label: Coverage Thresholds
nodes.rationale: Services/managers 80% (CI blocking), repositories 80% (blocking), agents 70% (blocking), IPC handlers 60% (warning), UI/components 50% (warning), E2E 100% of 3 main scenarios (blocking). Global min 70%. Coverage must not regress between PRs.

nodes.label: CI Blocking Rules
nodes.rationale: Lint/test failures block merging. E2E can be marked continue-on-error temporarily if unstable. Release blocked if tag-vs-version mismatch.

nodes.label: Release Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Triggered on semver tags. Matrix build on windows-latest, macos-latest, ubuntu-latest with fail-fast:false. Verifies tag matches package.json version. Builds/packages via electron-builder. Creates draft GitHub release. Uploads latest.json, latest*.yml, installers. Auto-detects pre-release via tag regex.

nodes.label: Code Signing Configuration
nodes.rationale: Windows Authenticode via CSC_LINK/CSC_KEY_PASSWORD secrets. macOS via APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, MAC_CSC_LINK. Graceful fallback: unsigned binaries if secrets absent; auto-update still works with security warnings.

nodes.label: Threat Model
nodes.rationale: Five threat actors: legitimate user, file-access attacker, network attacker, malicious plugin, compromised AI provider. Four asset categories: API keys, SQLite databases, source/translation files, logs.

nodes.label: Electron Sandbox Configuration
nodes.rationale: BrowserWindow with sandbox:true, contextIsolation:true, nodeIntegration:false, allowRunningInsecureContent:false, webSecurity:true. No app.enableSandbox(). This is the Electron 12+ security baseline.

nodes.label: Path Traversal Prevention
nodes.rationale: assertWithinProject(basePath, targetPath) validates all file paths. Resolves with path.resolve, verifies against authorized project directory. Rejects direct traversal (../), encoded traversal (%2e%2e), symlinks outside project, absolute paths, mixed separators on Windows.

nodes.label: API Key Storage Strategy
nodes.rationale: Tiered approach: Primary: keytar (OS keychain: Windows Credential Locker, macOS Keychain, Linux libsecret). Fallback: AES-256-GCM encrypted file at ~/.noveltrad/secrets.json. Keys never logged, never sent to renderer, never exposed to plugins. UI shows masked value (sk-...XXXX).

nodes.label: Content Security Policy
nodes.rationale: Strict CSP via session.defaultSession.webRequest.onHeadersReceived: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:.

nodes.label: Multi-layer Cache Strategy
nodes.rationale: Five cache types: AI responses (TTL 7 days, hash key), embeddings (SQLite, invalidated on model change), parse (file hash), TM (persistent SQLite), export (TTL 1 day). Global size limit 1GB with LRU eviction.

nodes.label: Worker Thread Parallelism
nodes.rationale: CPU-bound agents (parsing, consistency, export) run in Worker threads. Communication via parentPort.postMessage with JSON messages. Ollama concurrency limited to 1 job by default (maxConcurrentJobs). Promise.all for independent calls.

nodes.label: OOM Prevention Strategy
nodes.rationale: Hard limits: max source file 50MB, max paragraphs in memory 10,000, max AI batch 8,000 tokens. On OOM detection: reduce batch size and retry, clear temp caches after each job.

nodes.label: Performance Benchmarks
nodes.rationale: Target benchmarks: parsing 10K words <1s, translation short chapter <5min (7B model), export Markdown <1s, DOCX <3s, EPUB 50K words <5s, project open 100 chapters <2s.

nodes.label: CSS Design Tokens
nodes.rationale: Dark mode default (slate palette): bg-primary #0f172a, bg-secondary #1e293b, bg-tertiary #334155, text-primary #f8fafc, accent #38bdf8. Light mode inverts backgrounds. Theme switching via CSS custom properties and .theme-light class.

nodes.label: UI Component Library
nodes.rationale: Ten components: NtButton (default/hover/disabled/loading states), NtInput (default/focus/error/disabled), NtSelect, NtCard, NtSidebar (240px, active indicator), NtProgressBar (8px height), NtLogViewer (monospace, level coloring), NtDiffViewer (side-by-side/unified coloring), NtToast (top-right, 4s auto-dismiss), NtModal (overlay, focus trap, Escape close).

nodes.label: Accessibility (WCAG AA)
nodes.rationale: Minimum 4.5:1 text contrast, visible focus on interactive elements, ARIA labels for icons, documented keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+N new project, Ctrl+O open, Ctrl+Shift+T translate, Escape close).

nodes.label: 12-Sprint Plan
nodes.rationale: S1 Foundation, S2 UI, S3 Project management, S4 SQLite, S5 Ollama, S6 Workflow Engine, S7 AI Agents, S8 Lexicon+TM, S9 Consistency+Quality, S10 Export, S11 History+Auto-update, S12 Tests+Release. Each sprint 1-2 weeks with measurable deliverables.

nodes.label: MVP/Alpha/Beta/1.0 Milestones
nodes.rationale: MVP (S4 end): create project, import chapter, navigate. Alpha (S7 end): full workflow with 10 agents. Beta (S10 end): export + quality + history functional. 1.0 (S12 end): auto-update + E2E tests + public release.

nodes.label: Definition of Done
nodes.rationale: Code typed, unit tests pass, integration tests pass (if applicable), docs updated, code reviewed, no CI regression, UI-accessible (if visible feature).

nodes.label: Prompt Chaining Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Sequential agent chain: Split → Pre-translate → Translate (source + lexicon + TM) → Consistency → Lexicon → Grammar → Style → Polish → QA → Export. Each agent receives previousOutput. Split and Export are non-AI (regex/formatting).

nodes.label: AI Fallback Strategies
nodes.rationale: Four fallback scenarios: invalid JSON output (retry with json-fix prompt, max 2 attempts then mark failed), ethical refusal (reformulate as fictional analysis, 2 retries then switch provider), provider unavailable (3 retries with backoff then fallback provider), token limit exceeded (chunk text, process separately, reassemble).

nodes.label: Prompt Versioning
nodes.rationale: Prompts stored in prompts table (SQLite) with id, agent_id, version, role, content, created_at. Engine uses latest version unless user pins specific version. Frontmatter YAML: id, version, agent, role, language, target_model, output_format.

nodes.label: Model Compatibility Matrix
nodes.rationale: Primary model: qwen3.5:9b for all 8 AI agents. Fast alternative: qwen3.5:4b. Models <4B not recommended for JSON-output agents (consistency, lexicon, grammar, QA). Context window should be at least 2x chunk size.

nodes.label: OllamaManager Test Expansion
nodes.rationale: Phase 1: Expand OllamaManager tests to 90% coverage. Add tests for timeout, HTTP errors, invalid JSON, empty responses, streaming chunks, testModel errors, pullModel null reader, listModels HTTP error.

nodes.label: pnpm verify Command
nodes.rationale: Single validation command that runs sequentially: pnpm lint → pnpm type-check → pnpm test (Vitest) → pnpm test:e2e (Playwright) → pnpm build. Stops on first failure.

links.source_location: Section: Ce que NovelTrad doit retenir
